session_summary: Session completed full planning cycle for three-layer performance optimization. Cartography phase mapped 3 independent lanes: ToolCache, Parallel Dispatch, and WorkerPool. Architect synthesized combined plan. Critic reviewed with 5 blocking conditions, correcting the phase order. Plan artifact created at docs/json/opencode/handoffs/three-layer-perf-plan-handoff.v1.json. No implementation done — plan is repaired and ready for Phase 1 execution.
next_steps: Implement Phase 1 (ToolCache) with critic conditions applied. Details in the session prompt below.

findings.summary: ToolCache: wrap() in tool.ts is injection point. ScopedCache has inflight dedup. ~6 cacheable tools (read_source, grep, glob, read, read_artifact, registry). ExecuteResult is JSON-serializable. Blocker: wrap() has no cache service access.

Parallel dispatch: AI SDK streamText() already runs tools in parallel via Promise.all. Tool dispatch is INSIDE the AI SDK, not in opencode code. Quick win: 5-permit semaphore in tools.ts bridge. No ordering dependencies.

WorkerPool: Effect 4.0 ships unstable/workers with Worker<O,I>, RpcWorker, Schema-encoded init. Worker needs ~6 services vs ~60. Tool.Context NOT serializable — needs WorkerContext extraction. Shell tool (648 lines) is best candidate.
Three-layer plan: Phase 1 ToolCache (~120 lines), Phase 2 Parallel Dispatch (~5 lines), Phase 3 WorkerPool (~350 lines). Original order: Phase 2 → 1 → 3 by value-to-effort.

Verdict: REPAIR_REQUIRED. 5 blocking conditions: (1) Swap Phase 1 before Phase 2, (2) Add observable spans on semaphore + cache, (3) Add `cacheable` marker to Tool.Def, (4) 30s semaphore timeout, (5) Pin effect version for unstable/workers.

Phase order must be: ToolCache → Parallel Dispatch → WorkerPool. Cache hits reduce concurrent load, making semaphore tuning data-driven. Semaphore must have logging, spans, and timeout. Cache must emit hit/miss annotations. ToolCache should be opt-in via `cacheable` marker.
